title: Heat Conduction
text:
Auto-generated 2026-05-28 16:00 UTC  ·  5 plots
Quasi-2D steady heat-conduction compliance minimisation on a heated slab with SIMP material penalisation (p=3). The effective conductivity k_eff(ρ) = k_min + (k_max − k_min)·ρ³ controls heat routing; the compliance C = ∮_Γ q_n·T dΓ is the work done by the heat flux on the temperature field. The hot-spot boundary condition (central 1/3 stripe) breaks y-symmetry and drives topology optimisation toward non-trivial branching structures. Also supports source-identification experiments: recover the volumetric heat source f(x) from temperature observations via the identification_error = ||T − T_target||² objective.

title: Heat Conduction
text:
Initial Conditions
Visualisation of each initial condition available for this problem. IC plots are generated without running any solver.

Gaussian Source
Gaussian heat source centred at (cx·Lx, cy·Ly) = (0.5·Lx, 0.5·Ly) with width σ·min(Lx,Ly). Used as the control field for source-identification experiments (ic_field=‘source’ in physics dict).






NoteSettings





{
  "nx": 16,
  "ny": 8,
  "nz": 1,
  "amplitude": 1.0,
  "cx": 0.5,
  "cy": 0.5,
  "sigma": 0.2
}






Random
Gaussian-noise density field centred at ρ₀=0.5 (σ=0.3, clipped to [0.05, 0.95]); breaks spatial symmetry to produce non-trivial per-cell gradient sensitivity maps.






NoteSettings





{
  "rho_0": 0.5,
  "noise": 0.3,
  "nx": 16,
  "ny": 8,
  "nz": 1,
  "seed": 0
}






Two Gaussians
Two-Gaussian volumetric heat source at (0.3·Lx, 0.5·Ly) and (0.7·Lx, 0.5·Ly). Ground-truth source for source-recovery experiments.

Uniform SIMP thermal conductivity density ρ₀ over all hex mesh elements; standard homogeneous starting point for heat-conduction topology optimisation.

title: Navier–Stokes (2D)
text:
Auto-generated 2026-05-28 16:00 UTC  ·  26 plots
2D incompressible Navier–Stokes on a doubly-periodic domain with viscosity ν as the primary control parameter. The nonlinear advection term ∇·(u⊗u) transfers energy across scales; at low ν the flow develops turbulent cascades and the Lyapunov exponent grows, making long-horizon gradients exponentially sensitive to perturbations.

title: Navier–Stokes (2D)
text:
Initial Conditions
Visualisation of each initial condition available for this problem. IC plots are generated without running any solver.

Flat Inflow
Flat 1-D inlet velocity profile u_x(y) = U, shape (N,). Starting point for inflow-profile drag-optimisation experiments.






NoteSettings





{
  "N": 64,
  "U": 0.5
}






Multimode
Incompressible velocity field with energy concentrated in a ring at wavenumber k=2 (σ_k=0.5); supports multi-scale turbulent development.






NoteSettings





{
  "N": 64
}






Tgv
Taylor–Green vortex u=sin(x)cos(y), v=−cos(x)sin(y); has a closed-form analytic solution for viscous decay, enabling solver verification.






NoteSettings





{
  "N": 64
}






Uniform
Uniform rightward flow u=(U, 0) — background flow for cylinder-wake (Kármán vortex street) experiments. Obstacle specified separately via the physics.obstacle field.






NoteSettings





{
  "N": 64,
  "U": 1.0
}

title: Navier–Stokes (2D)
Navier–Stokes (2D)
Navier–Stokes (2D)
Navier–Stokes (3D)
text:
Auto-generated 2026-05-28 16:00 UTC  ·  7 plots
3D incompressible Navier–Stokes on a triply-periodic domain with viscosity ν as the primary control parameter. The 3D extension admits helical structures, vortex stretching, and faster chaos onset than 2D: chaos horizon T* ≈ 8–16 s vs T* > 64 s in 2D (at ν=0.001, N=16). Gradient norms grow (vortex stretching) rather than decaying as in 2D.

title: Navier–Stokes (3D)

Structural Mechanics
text:
Auto-generated 2026-05-28 16:00 UTC  ·  3 plots
3D linear-elasticity compliance minimisation on a cantilever beam with SIMP material penalisation (p=3, E_max=70 000 MPa). The stiffness matrix K(ρ) couples every density element to the global displacement field via the constitutive relation E_eff(ρ) = E_min + (E_max − E_min)·ρ³; the compliance objective C = F^T K(ρ)⁻¹ F is smooth but non-convex in ρ, driving gradient-based topology optimisation toward sparse binary 0/1 layouts.

title: Structural Mechanics
text:
Initial Conditions
Visualisation of each initial condition available for this problem. IC plots are generated without running any solver.

Random
Gaussian-noise density field centred at ρ₀=0.5 (σ=0.3, clipped to [0.05, 0.95]); breaks spatial symmetry so gradient experiments see non-trivial per-cell sensitivity.






NoteSettings





{}






Two Density Bumps
Ground-truth density with two stiff Gaussian pillars (ρ_peak=0.95, σ=0.12·min(Lx,Lz)) at (0.35·Lx, 0.5·Ly, 0.5·Lz) and (0.75·Lx, 0.5·Ly, 0.5·Lz) on a soft ρ_bg=0.1 background; analog of thermal-mesh two_gaussians for the load-recovery inverse experiment (recover density from displacement observations).






NoteSettings





{
  "nx": 16,
  "ny": 2,
  "nz": 8
}






Uniform
Uniform SIMP material density ρ₀ over all hex mesh elements; standard homogeneous starting point for topology optimisation of the cantilever beam.

  "nx": 16
